note: as_of 2024-05-15 is a trading day (Wednesday), so the last settled close <= as_of is on the as_of date itself: 82.75. No stock split between as_of and the re-fetch date, so split_factor = 1 and the contemporaneous NOMINAL close equals Yahoo raw_close(last <= as_of) = 82.75. Price re-derived two independent ways: (1) Yahoo v8 query1 raw_close x split_factor 1 = 82.75, (2) Yahoo v8 query2 endpoint = 82.75; the committed local quote CSV (S006, backtests/data/asof_2024-05-15_hwm_quotes.csv) carries the same 82.75 close. IMPORTANT - DELIBERATE NEAR-HIGH ENTRY: the as_of close sits ~0.1% below the 52-week intraday high ($82.84) because HWM popped +15.5% on 2024-05-02 on a RAISED full-year guide and drifted up to the as_of date. This is a genuine near-52-week-high entry, NOT a data error (it is not the wrong extreme of the series - it is literally the last close). The T2 low/high-hug tripwire is therefore JUSTIFIED: the decision explicitly treats the rich, at-the-high entry as the binding constraint (STARTER not CORE; buy-below set ~$72, well under the high). low_high_hug_justified is set true with that reasoning. The entry is +96.2% off the 52-week low ($42.18) and -0.1% off the 52-week high ($82.84).

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Deliberate near-52-week-high entry: $82.75 is the actual last settled close on the as_of trading day (2024-05-15), ~0.1% below the 52wk intraday high ($82.84), after a +15.5% Q1'24 earnings pop on a RAISED guide. This is the correct last close, not a grabbed extreme. The richness IS the thesis's binding constraint - the card treats it as such (STARTER not CORE; buy-below ~$72). Justified.
